title: Common Mistakes in Self Introduction — Complete Error Analysis
description: 100+ common mistakes that Indian English learners make during self-introduction, with corrections and detailed explanations.

mistakes.correct: My name is Rahul Sharma. / I am Rahul Sharma.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ खुद Rahul Sharma। → ✅ मेरा नाम Rahul Sharma है।
mistakes.explanation: 'Myself' is a reflexive pronoun. It cannot be used to introduce yourself directly. 'Myself Rahul' is a very common Indian English mistake. Always use 'My name is' or 'I am' or 'I'm'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Myself' एक reflexive pronoun है। इसे directly introduction के लिए use नहीं किया जा सकता। 'Myself Rahul' बहुत common Indian English mistake है।
mistakes.tip: Never start with 'Myself'. Always say 'My name is' or 'I am'.

mistakes.correct: My name is Rahul. / People call me Rahul.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मुझे Rahul कहा जाता है। → ✅ मेरा नाम Rahul है।
mistakes.explanation: 'I am called' is passive and unnatural in self-introduction. Use 'My name is' for formal settings or 'People call me' for nicknames.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'I am called' passive है और self-introduction में unnatural लगता है।
mistakes.tip: Use 'I am called' only when reporting what others call you. For direct introduction, use 'My name is'.

mistakes.correct: My name is Priya.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मेरा नाम Priya है। (Incorrect usage of 'having') → ✅ My name is Priya.
mistakes.explanation: 'Have' is a stative verb and should not be used in continuous tense for possession. 'I am having' is incorrect — use simple present 'I have' or 'My name is'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Have' stative verb है — इसका continuous form 'am having' possession के लिए गलत है।
mistakes.tip: Never use 'am having' for possession — use 'have' or 'My name is'.

mistakes.correct: My name is Suresh.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मेरा अच्छा नाम Suresh है। → ✅ My name is Suresh.
mistakes.explanation: 'Good name' is an Indian English expression directly translated from Hindi 'aapka shubh naam'. In standard English, it is simply 'My name is'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Good name' Hindi के 'शुभ नाम' का direct translation है। Standard English में सिर्फ 'My name is' use होता है।
mistakes.tip: There is no concept of 'good name' in English. Just say 'My name is'.

mistakes.correct: My name is Arun.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं नाम Arun से संबंध रखता हूँ। → ✅ My name is Arun.
mistakes.explanation: 'Belong to' is used for origin/place, not for names. You 'belong to Delhi', not 'belong to a name'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Belong to' place के लिए use होता है, नाम के लिए नहीं।
mistakes.tip: Use 'belong to' only for place or organization, not for name.

mistakes.correct: I belong to Delhi. / I am from Delhi.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं दिल्ली से संबंध हूँ। → ✅ I belong to Delhi / I am from Delhi.
mistakes.explanation: 'Belong' is a main verb here — do not add 'am' before it. 'I am belong' is incorrect grammar. Say 'I belong to' or 'I am from'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Belong' यहाँ main verb है — इससे पहले 'am' नहीं लगाते। 'I am belong' grammatically गलत है।
mistakes.tip: Never say 'I am belong to'. Say 'I belong to' or 'I am from'.

mistakes.correct: I am from Delhi.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं the दिल्ली से हूँ। → ✅ I am from Delhi.
mistakes.explanation: Do not use 'the' before city names. Cities, towns, and countries generally do not take 'the'. Exception: 'the USA', 'the UK', 'the Netherlands'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: City names के पहले 'the' नहीं लगाते। Cities generally 'the' नहीं लेती।
mistakes.tip: No 'the' before city names: Delhi, Mumbai, London, New York.

mistakes.correct: I live in Mumbai. / I stay in Mumbai.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं Mumbai में stay हूँ। → ✅ I live in Mumbai / I stay in Mumbai.
mistakes.explanation: 'I am stay' is grammatically wrong — 'stay' is a verb that needs to be in correct form. Say 'I stay' or better 'I live in' for permanent residence.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'I am stay' grammatically गलत है। 'stay' verb को correct form चाहिए। Permanent residence के लिए 'I live in' बेहतर है।
mistakes.tip: 'I live in' for permanent residence. 'I stay in' also acceptable but slightly informal.

mistakes.correct: I hail from Kolkata. / I am from Kolkata.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं Kolkata से hailing हूँ। → ✅ I hail from Kolkata.
mistakes.explanation: 'Hail' as a stative verb of origin should not be in continuous form. Say 'I hail from' not 'I am hailing from'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Hail from' origin के लिए use होता है — इसका continuous form गलत है।
mistakes.tip: Use 'I hail from' (not 'I am hailing from') in formal contexts.

mistakes.correct: I was born in Pune.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं Pune में पैदा हूँ। → ✅ I was born in Pune.
mistakes.explanation: Birth is a completed event that happened in the past. Use Past Simple: 'I WAS born'. Not 'I am born'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: जन्म एक completed past event है। इसलिए Past Tense 'I was born' use होता है।
mistakes.tip: Always use 'I WAS born in' — never 'I am born in'.

mistakes.correct: I have completed my B.Tech. / I completed my B.Tech.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं B.Tech done हूँ। → ✅ I have completed / I completed my B.Tech.
mistakes.explanation: 'I am done' is very informal and not grammatically correct as an introduction. Use 'I have completed' (Present Perfect) or 'I completed' (Past Simple).
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'I am done' informal और grammatically गलत है formal introduction में। 'I have completed' या 'I completed' use करें।
mistakes.tip: For education: 'I completed my degree from...' or 'I have completed my degree from...'

mistakes.wrong: I am doing job in TCS.
mistakes.correct: I work at TCS. / I am working at TCS.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं TCS में job कर रहा हूँ। → ✅ I work at TCS / I am working at TCS.
mistakes.explanation: 'Doing job' is not correct English. The correct phrases are 'I work at', 'I am employed at', 'I work with', or 'I am working at'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Doing job' correct English नहीं है। 'I work at' या 'I am working at' use करें।
mistakes.tip: Never say 'doing job'. Say 'I work at' or 'I am employed at'.

mistakes.correct: I am a software engineer. / I work in software.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं एक software हूँ। → ✅ I am a software engineer.
mistakes.explanation: You are a person, not a technology. Always include the profession title: 'software engineer', 'software developer', 'software analyst'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: आप एक person हैं, technology नहीं। हमेशा full profession title use करें।
mistakes.tip: Complete the sentence: 'software engineer', 'software developer', 'software analyst'.

mistakes.wrong: I have done engineering from Delhi.
mistakes.correct: I completed my engineering degree from Delhi University. / I studied engineering at Delhi University.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैंने दिल्ली से engineering done है। → ✅ I completed my engineering degree from Delhi University.
mistakes.explanation: Specify the type of degree and full institution name. 'Done engineering from Delhi' is too vague and grammatically informal.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Degree type और पूरे institution name को specify करें।
mistakes.tip: Always say: 'I completed my + [degree type] from + [full institution name] in + [year]'.

mistakes.correct: I have been working for 3 years.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं 3 साल से working हूँ। → ✅ I have been working for 3 years.
mistakes.explanation: For duration from past to now, use Present Perfect Continuous: 'I have been + verb+ing + for + time period'. Use 'for' (not 'since') with a period of time (3 years). Use 'since' with a point in time (since 2020).
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Past से present तक की duration के लिए Present Perfect Continuous use होता है। 'Since' specific time के साथ, 'for' duration के साथ।
mistakes.tip: FOR + duration: 'for 3 years', 'for a month'. SINCE + point: 'since 2020', 'since January'.

mistakes.wrong: I am working from past 2 years.
mistakes.correct: I have been working for the past 2 years.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं past 2 years से working हूँ। → ✅ I have been working for the past 2 years.
mistakes.explanation: Same issue as above: 'working from' is wrong. Use 'have been working for' + the word 'past' before the number.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Working from' गलत है। 'Have been working for the past 2 years' सही है।
mistakes.tip: Pattern: I have been + verb+ing + for the past + [number] + [time unit]

mistakes.correct: I hold a Bachelor of Commerce degree. / I completed my B.Com from XYZ College.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मेरी qualification B.Com है। → ✅ I hold a Bachelor of Commerce degree.
mistakes.explanation: 'My qualification is' is technically not wrong, but it sounds unnatural in spoken introduction. More natural phrases are 'I hold a degree in' or 'I completed my B.Com from'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'My qualification is' technically गलत नहीं है, लेकिन spoken introduction में unnatural लगता है।
mistakes.tip: More natural: 'I hold a degree in' or 'I completed my B.Com from + [College] in + [Year]'.

categories.title: Hobby and Interest Mistakes
categories.hindiTitle: Hobby और रुचि बताने में गलतियाँ

mistakes.wrong: My hobby are reading and cooking.
mistakes.correct: My hobbies are reading and cooking. / My hobby is reading.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मेरे hobby reading और cooking हैं। → ✅ My hobbies are reading and cooking.
mistakes.explanation: Subject-verb agreement: When there are two hobbies, use plural 'My hobbies ARE'. When there is one, use 'My hobby IS'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Subject-verb agreement: दो hobbies = 'My hobbies ARE'. एक hobby = 'My hobby IS'.
mistakes.tip: One hobby: 'My hobby is playing cricket'. Multiple hobbies: 'My hobbies are playing cricket and reading'.

mistakes.wrong: I like to play cricket and to read books.
mistakes.correct: I like playing cricket and reading books.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मुझे cricket खेलना और books पढ़ना पसंद है (infinitive) → ✅ I like playing cricket and reading books (gerund).
mistakes.explanation: After 'like', 'love', 'enjoy', 'prefer' (when expressing habits), use Gerund (-ing form), not infinitive. Both are actually acceptable, but gerund sounds more natural for hobbies.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Like', 'love', 'enjoy' के बाद gerund (-ing) ज्यादा natural लगती है।
mistakes.tip: I love/enjoy/like + verb+ing: 'I enjoy reading', 'I love playing', 'I like cooking'.

mistakes.wrong: I am fond of to travel.
mistakes.correct: I am fond of travelling. / I love travelling.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मुझे travel करना पसंद है (preposition + infinitive) → ✅ I am fond of travelling.
mistakes.explanation: After preposition 'of', always use Gerund (-ing): 'fond of travelling', 'interested in reading', 'good at cooking'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Preposition 'of' के बाद हमेशा Gerund (-ing) आती है।
mistakes.tip: Preposition + Gerund rule: 'fond OF travel-LING', 'interested IN read-ING', 'good AT cook-ING'.

mistakes.correct: I am interested in music. / I have an interest in music.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मुझे music में interested हूँ (wrong preposition) → ✅ I am interested IN music.
mistakes.explanation: The correct preposition after 'interested' is 'IN', not 'to'. 'I am interested in + noun/gerund'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Interested' के बाद preposition 'IN' आती है, 'TO' नहीं।
mistakes.tip: Always: 'interested IN + noun/gerund'. Never: 'interested TO'.

mistakes.correct: I have an interest in singing. / I am interested in singing.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मुझे singing में interest है (article missing) → ✅ I have AN interest in singing.
mistakes.explanation: When using 'interest' as a noun, it needs the article 'an': 'have an interest in'. Alternatively, use the adjective form: 'I am interested in'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Noun 'interest' के पहले article 'an' जरूरी है। या adjective form 'interested in' use करें।
mistakes.tip: Noun form: 'I have AN interest in' | Adjective form: 'I am interested in'

mistakes.correct: My family has 4 members. / There are 4 members in my family.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मेरा परिवार 4 members का है। → ✅ My family has 4 members.
mistakes.explanation: Two correct ways: (1) 'My family has + number + members' using 'have' for possession. (2) 'There are + number + members in my family' using existential 'there'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: दो सही तरीके: (1) 'My family has + number + members' (2) 'There are + number + members in my family'
mistakes.tip: Most natural: 'My family has 4 members' or 'There are 4 of us in the family'.

mistakes.wrong: My father is doing a job in bank.
mistakes.correct: My father works at a bank. / My father is a bank employee.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मेरे पिता bank में job करते हैं। → ✅ My father works at a bank.
mistakes.explanation: 'Doing a job' is not natural English. Use 'works at/in' for place of work. Also, use the article 'a' before 'bank'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Doing a job' natural English नहीं है। 'Works at/in' use करें।
mistakes.tip: Never 'doing job'. Use 'works at', 'works for', 'is employed at'.

mistakes.correct: My brother is an engineer.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मेरे भाई engineer हैं। (article missing) → ✅ My brother is AN engineer.
mistakes.explanation: Before singular countable nouns starting with a vowel sound, use 'an'. Engineer starts with vowel sound 'e', so 'an engineer' is correct.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Vowel sound से शुरू होने वाले singular countable nouns के पहले 'an' लगता है।
mistakes.tip: A/An rule: 'a doctor', 'an engineer', 'a teacher', 'an architect', 'an accountant'.

mistakes.correct: I have been married for 3 years.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं 3 साल से married हूँ (wrong tense + wrong preposition) → ✅ I have been married for 3 years.
mistakes.explanation: For ongoing states from past to present, use Present Perfect or Present Perfect Continuous. Use 'for' + duration. 'I have been married for 3 years' is correct.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Past से present तक की ongoing state के लिए Present Perfect use करें। 'For' + duration।
mistakes.tip: State ongoing since past: 'I have been married for' / 'I have been living here for'

mistakes.wrong: I am having a wife and two childrens.
mistakes.correct: I have a wife and two children.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मेरी wife और दो children हैं (wrong continuous + wrong plural) → ✅ I have a wife and two children.
mistakes.explanation: Two errors: (1) 'Am having' for possession is wrong — use 'I have'. (2) 'Childrens' is wrong — 'children' is already plural. Correct: 'I have a wife and two children.'
mistakes.hindiExplanation: दो errors: (1) Possession के लिए 'I have' use करें, 'am having' नहीं। (2) 'Children' already plural है — 'childrens' गलत है।
mistakes.tip: Irregular plural: child → children (NOT childrens). Also: man→men, woman→women, mouse→mice.

mistakes.wrong: I am very much glad to meet you.
mistakes.correct: I am very glad to meet you. / I am so glad to meet you.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मुझे आपसे बहुत much खुशी है। → ✅ I am very glad to meet you.
mistakes.explanation: Do not use 'very much' before an adjective. Use 'very' or 'so' before adjectives. 'Very much' is used before verbs: 'I very much appreciate it.'
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Adjective से पहले 'very much' नहीं लगाते। 'Very' या 'so' लगाते हैं। 'Very much' verbs के साथ use होता है।
mistakes.tip: Before adjectives: 'very glad', 'so happy'. Before verbs: 'very much appreciate', 'very much enjoy'.

mistakes.correct: Nice to meet you.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ Nice to meet to you. → ✅ Nice to meet you.
mistakes.explanation: The phrase is simply 'Nice to meet YOU'. There is no 'to' before 'you'. Common error caused by Hindi thinking pattern.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Correct phrase: 'Nice to meet YOU'. 'You' से पहले 'to' नहीं आता।
mistakes.tip: Memorize: 'Nice to meet YOU' — no extra 'to'.

mistakes.wrong: How are you? I am fine, thank you.
mistakes.correct: I am doing well, thank you. / I am great, thank you! / Pretty good, thanks!
mistakes.hindi: ❌ I am fine, thank you (too formal/old-fashioned) → ✅ I am doing well / I am great
mistakes.explanation: 'I am fine' is technically not wrong, but it sounds old-fashioned and emotionless in modern English. More natural responses: 'I am doing well', 'I am great', 'Pretty good, thanks', 'Not bad, thanks!'
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'I am fine' technically गलत नहीं है, लेकिन modern English में old-fashioned और emotionless लगता है।
mistakes.tip: Better responses: 'Doing great!', 'Really well, thanks!', 'Pretty good, and you?'

mistakes.correct: I am an IT professional.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ I am a IT professional. → ✅ I am AN IT professional.
mistakes.explanation: Before words starting with vowel SOUNDS, use 'an'. 'IT' is pronounced 'Eye-Tee' — starts with vowel sound 'I'. So: 'an IT professional', 'an MBA degree', 'an HR manager'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Vowel sound से शुरू होने वाले words के पहले 'an' use होता है। 'IT' = 'Eye-Tee' — vowel sound 'I' से शुरू।
mistakes.tip: Sound rule, not spelling rule: 'an hour', 'a university', 'an MBA', 'an HR', 'a European'.

mistakes.wrong: I am working at the TCS.
mistakes.correct: I am working at TCS.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ I work at the TCS. → ✅ I work at TCS.
mistakes.explanation: Do not use 'the' before company names when they are used as proper nouns. Exception: 'The Times of India', 'The Guardian' (newspapers often use 'the').
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Company names के पहले 'the' नहीं लगाते (generally).
mistakes.tip: No 'the' before: TCS, Infosys, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Flipkart.

mistakes.wrong: I am an engineer at an MNC company.
mistakes.correct: I am an engineer at an MNC.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ MNC company (redundant) → ✅ MNC only
mistakes.explanation: 'MNC' stands for 'Multi-National Company' — 'C' already means 'Company'. Saying 'MNC company' is redundant (like saying 'ATM machine' or 'PIN number').
mistakes.hindiExplanation: MNC = Multi-National Company — 'C' already 'Company' है। 'MNC company' redundant है।
mistakes.tip: Avoid redundancy: 'MNC' (not 'MNC company'), 'ATM' (not 'ATM machine'), 'PIN' (not 'PIN number').

mistakes.correct: I am a 'SOFT-ware' engineer.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ soff-tware (wrong pronunciation) → ✅ SOFT-ware (correct stress)
mistakes.explanation: Correct pronunciation: 'SOFTWARE' — stress on 'SOFT'. Not 'soff-ware'. The 't' in 'soft' should be clearly pronounced.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Correct: 'SOFT-ware' — stress 'SOFT' पर। 't' clearly pronounce करें।
mistakes.tip: Software: SOFT-ware (t sound) | Hardware: HARD-ware | Firmware: FIRM-ware

mistakes.correct: Speak at a moderate pace. Use pauses after key points.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ बहुत जल्दी-जल्दी बोलना → ✅ Moderate pace से बोलना, key points के बाद pause लेना
mistakes.explanation: Many students rush through their introduction out of nervousness. Pauses are powerful — they make you look confident and give listeners time to absorb information.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Nervousness से बहुत जल्दी बोलना common mistake है। Pauses confidence दिखाती हैं।
mistakes.tip: Pause rule: After your name, pause. After your city, pause. Gives authority to your words.

mistakes.correct: Smooth, filler-free introduction with deliberate pauses.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ Um, err, so, basically, actually का ज्यादा use → ✅ Clean, filler-free speech
mistakes.explanation: Fillers (um, err, so, basically, actually) used excessively make you appear unprepared. Practice until your introduction flows naturally without fillers.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: बहुत ज्यादा fillers (um, err, basically) आपको unprepared दिखाते हैं।
mistakes.tip: Replace fillers with deliberate pauses. Practice until the words come naturally.

mistakes.wrong: Not making eye contact, looking at floor/ceiling.
mistakes.correct: Maintain warm eye contact — look at the person or camera.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ नीचे/ऊपर देखते हुए बोलना → ✅ Eye contact बनाए रखना
mistakes.explanation: Eye contact is crucial for confidence. When speaking to one person, maintain soft eye contact. In a group, make eye contact with different people in the room.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Eye contact confidence के लिए crucial है। One person — soft eye contact। Group — अलग-अलग लोगों के साथ eye contact।
mistakes.tip: For camera/screen: look AT the camera lens, not at your own face on screen.

mistakes.correct: I like this company.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मुझे यह company like हो रही है। → ✅ I like this company.
mistakes.explanation: Stative verbs (like, love, hate, know, believe, understand, want, need, prefer) do NOT use continuous form. 'I am liking' is incorrect. Use simple form: 'I like'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Stative verbs (like, love, hate, know) का continuous form use नहीं होता। 'I am liking' गलत है।
mistakes.tip: Stative verbs — NO continuous: like, love, hate, know, believe, understand, want, need, seem, appear.

mistakes.wrong: I am knowing many people here.
mistakes.correct: I know many people here.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं यहाँ बहुत लोगों को knowing हूँ। → ✅ I know many people here.
mistakes.explanation: 'Know' is a stative verb — it does not use continuous form. Say 'I know' not 'I am knowing'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Know' stative verb है — 'I am knowing' गलत है। 'I know' correct है।
mistakes.tip: 'Know' = stative verb. Always simple tense: 'I know', 'I knew', 'I will know'.

mistakes.wrong: I have joined this company 2 years back.
mistakes.correct: I joined this company 2 years ago.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ 2 years back (wrong phrase) → ✅ 2 years AGO
mistakes.explanation: 'Back' is not the correct word for past time in formal English. Use 'AGO': '2 years ago', '3 months ago', 'a week ago'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Back' past time के लिए formal English में use नहीं होता। 'AGO' correct है।
mistakes.tip: Always: '2 years AGO', '3 months AGO'. Not 'back'.

mistakes.wrong: I have joined this company in 2022.
mistakes.correct: I joined this company in 2022.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ I have joined in 2022. → ✅ I joined in 2022.
mistakes.explanation: When a specific past time (year, date) is mentioned, use Simple Past NOT Present Perfect. 'I joined in 2022' (simple past). Present Perfect is used for unspecified past: 'I have joined recently'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Specific past time (year) के साथ Simple Past use होता है, Present Perfect नहीं।
mistakes.tip: With time words (in 2022, last year, yesterday): use Simple Past. Without time: can use Present Perfect.

mistakes.correct: I am working at Google. / I work for Google.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ I work in Google. → ✅ I work AT Google / I work FOR Google.
mistakes.explanation: For specific companies and organizations, use 'at' (physical location) or 'for' (employment relationship). 'In' is used for industries/sectors: 'I work in IT', 'I work in finance'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Specific company: 'at' (location) या 'for' (employment)। Industry/sector: 'in IT', 'in finance'.
mistakes.tip: Company/organization: work AT/FOR. Industry/sector: work IN. 'I work AT TCS' | 'I work IN the IT sector'.

mistakes.correct: I am from Rajasthan.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ Rajasthan state (state redundant) → ✅ I am from Rajasthan.
mistakes.explanation: When you say the name of the state/city/country, you do not need to add the category word (state, city, country) after it. 'Rajasthan' is already the name of a state — no need to say 'Rajasthan state'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: State/city का नाम लेने के बाद 'state', 'city', 'country' add करने की जरूरत नहीं।
mistakes.tip: Just 'Rajasthan', not 'Rajasthan state'. Just 'Delhi', not 'Delhi city'. Just 'India', not 'India country'.

mistakes.wrong: I passed out from IIT in 2021.
mistakes.correct: I graduated from IIT in 2021.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ passed out (Indian English) → ✅ graduated from
mistakes.explanation: 'Passed out' in Indian English means 'graduated', but in standard English, 'passed out' means 'lost consciousness' (fainted). Always use 'graduated from' to avoid confusion.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Indian English में 'passed out' = graduated है। लेकिन standard English में 'passed out' = बेहोश होना। हमेशा 'graduated from' use करें।
mistakes.tip: Indian English 'passed out' can cause confusion. Always use 'graduated from'.

categories.title: Common Indian English Expressions to Avoid
categories.hindiTitle: Common Indian English Expressions — जो Avoid करनी चाहिए

mistakes.wrong: I am doing the job of manager.
mistakes.correct: I work as a manager. / I am a manager.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ मैं manager का job कर रहा हूँ। → ✅ I work as a manager.
mistakes.explanation: Direct translation from Hindi 'mai manager ka kaam karta hun' results in 'I am doing the job of manager' — unnatural in English. Use 'I work as' or 'I am a'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Hindi ka direct translation — unnatural English banta hai.
mistakes.tip: Professional role: 'I work AS a manager' / 'I AM a manager'.

mistakes.wrong: I am in a government job.
mistakes.correct: I work for the government. / I am a government employee.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ I am in government job. → ✅ I work for the government.
mistakes.explanation: 'In a government job' is acceptable in Indian English, but more standard is 'I work for the government' or 'I am a government employee'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Work for the government' ya 'government employee' zyada standard hai.
mistakes.tip: More standard: 'I work for the government' or 'I am employed in the public sector'.

mistakes.wrong: I am the topper of my class.
mistakes.correct: I was the top student in my class. / I ranked first in my class.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ I am the topper (Indian English). → ✅ I was the top student in my class.
mistakes.explanation: 'Topper' is pure Indian English. In standard English, say 'I was the top student', 'I ranked first', 'I received the highest marks', 'I graduated with distinction'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Topper' pure Indian English है। Standard English: 'top student', 'ranked first'.
mistakes.tip: 'Topper' = Indian English. Standard English: 'top student', 'ranked first', 'highest scorer'.

mistakes.correct: I am very excited. / I am so excited.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ very much excited → ✅ very excited / so excited
mistakes.explanation: 'Very much' is used before verbs and verb phrases, not before adjectives. 'I appreciate it very much' (verb). 'I am very excited' (adjective — no 'much').
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Very much' adjectives के पहले use नहीं होता — verbs के पहले होता है।
mistakes.tip: Before adjectives: 'very excited', 'so happy'. NOT 'very much excited'. Before verbs: 'very much appreciate'.

mistakes.correct: I will get back to you. / I will respond to you.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ revert (Indian English for 'respond') → ✅ get back to you / respond
mistakes.explanation: 'Revert' in Indian business English is used to mean 'respond/reply', but in standard English, 'revert' means 'to return to a previous state'. Use 'get back to you' or 'respond'.
mistakes.hindiExplanation: Indian business English में 'revert' = respond/reply, लेकिन standard English में 'revert' = previous state पर वापस जाना।
mistakes.tip: 'I will revert' = Indian English. Standard: 'I will get back to you', 'I will respond', 'I will reply'.

mistakes.correct: Move the meeting earlier. / Reschedule the meeting to an earlier time.
mistakes.hindi: ❌ prepone (Indian English) → ✅ move earlier / reschedule to an earlier time
mistakes.explanation: 'Prepone' does not exist in standard English dictionaries. The opposite of 'postpone' is 'move earlier' or 'bring forward' (British English).
mistakes.hindiExplanation: 'Prepone' standard English में exist नहीं करता। 'Postpone' का opposite 'move earlier' या 'bring forward' है।
mistakes.tip: Postpone = delay, push back. 'Prepone' = NOT a word. Use: 'move earlier', 'bring forward', 'reschedule to an earlier time'.

quickRevisionList.title: Top 20 Mistakes to Remember
list: ❌ Myself Rahul → ✅ My name is Rahul
❌ My good name is → ✅ My name is
❌ I am born in → ✅ I was born in
❌ I am belong to → ✅ I belong to / I am from
❌ I am doing job → ✅ I work at
❌ I am working since 3 years → ✅ I have been working for 3 years
❌ My hobby are → ✅ My hobbies are
❌ interested to music → ✅ interested IN music
❌ I am having a wife → ✅ I have a wife
❌ two childrens → ✅ two children
❌ I am liking → ✅ I like
❌ 2 years back → ✅ 2 years ago
❌ passed out from → ✅ graduated from
❌ I work in Google → ✅ I work AT Google
❌ a IT professional → ✅ an IT professional
❌ very much excited → ✅ very excited
❌ I am done my B.Tech → ✅ I completed my B.Tech
❌ I am stay in Mumbai → ✅ I live in Mumbai
❌ I have joined in 2022 → ✅ I joined in 2022
❌ Nice to meet to you → ✅ Nice to meet you
